zotero.abstractNote: We propose sparse reduced-rank regression (SRRR) as a method for exploratory visualisation of paired multivariate data. SRRR finds a low-dimensional projection of the response variables that is maximally predictable from the predictor variables, while enforcing sparsity on the projection weights. This allows for identifying a small subset of predictor variables that are most relevant for explaining the variation in the response variables. We demonstrate the utility of SRRR on several real-world datasets, including gene expression data and neural activity data, and show that it provides more interpretable visualisations than standard reduced-rank regression or canonical correlation analysis.
